rulings.Case Title: CARROLL CONNOR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, March 24, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs¿ discovery responses are factually devoid with respect to asbestos content. Plaintiffs¿ evidence fails to create a triable issue whether the insulation and gasket material Mr. Conner alleges exposure to contained asbestos. The declaration of Charles Ay lacks foundation for his conclusion that the insulation and gasket material in question more likely than not contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to the statements in Mr. Baker¿s declaration regarding asbestos content are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD PALMER VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Corporation Erroneously Sued Herein As Pneumo-Abexs' Motion For Reconsideration (Of Its Order Dated January 28,2010)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, March 24, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Successor In Interest To Abex Corporation Erroneously Sued Herein As Pneumo-Abexs' motion for reconsideration of the denial of its motion for summary adjudication on January 28, 2010 is denied. Defendant failed to present ¿new or different facts, circumstance, or law¿ that warrant reconsideration. CCP 1008(a). The denial of defendant¿s prior motion is not inconsistent with the granting of Dana Company¿s motion for summary adjudication. In Dana¿s motion the plaintiff lacked defendant-specific evidence sufficient to warrant a trial on the issue of punitive damages. By contrast, in Pneumo Abex¿s motion plaintiff¿s responses to special interrogatories contained several facts specific to the defendant and Barry Castleman had a foundation upon which to base his opinion that defendant was aware of the hazards of asbestos. =(220/HEK)
